summary: Applied the minimal lint-only repair requested by lead gate: removed the unused parseSiegeId import and removed the useless aiRejectionCount increment-before-throw path by making the counter a fixed zero value. No functional behavior changed. The multi-year AI runner remains deterministic, stays on the same GameCommand path as the player-facing API, and still reports the previously documented live no-action-only limitation without introducing telemetry, analytics, server, multiplayer, arbitrary-code AI scripting, personality simulation, or full diplomacy AI.
decisions: Keep the repair strictly lint-only; do not modify the multi-year AI run behavior or widen the acceptance surface.
Remove dead code instead of masking the lint warning with suppression comments.
Preserve the existing deterministic evidence and the documented live no-action-only limitation unchanged.
risks: The previously documented live multi-year run still reports zero accepted AI commands because the accepted slice yields no actionable live candidates; this was not changed by the lint-only repair.
The branch still contains predecessor-authored task/state/message churn, but this repair did not expand beyond the allowed scope.
requested_action: Review the lint-only repair and confirm it is safe to route onward to lead_orchestrator.
